title: Boombox
alt: And she's gonna feel like a jerk when she realizes it was actually Under Pressure.
transcript:
[Megan is looking out a second story window at Cueball holding a boombox over his head. Musical Notes are coming out of it.]
Cueball: Megan!
Megan: Oh my god, I can't believe this is happening.
Cueball: I love you!
Megan: Okay, that's great. Wait a second. Is... is that... Ice Ice Baby? What the hell?
Cueball: I'm not very good at this.
explanation:
We see Cueball declare his love for Megan in an oft-used setting, paying homage to similar events in classic literature, notably the "balcony scene" from  play  (act 2, scene 2), and a similar situation in 's  (act 3, scenes 6-7). In the former, Romeo, attempting to woo Juliet, stands beneath her balcony to profess his love for her. In the latter, an inarticulate cadet, Christian, professes his love for Roxane by arranging to use the words of a fellow soldier, Cyrano, who secretly also loves Roxane. The 1989 movie "" contains a modern interpretation of this declaration of love, where  plays  "" on a boombox outside the house of the girl he likes.

Parodying this iconic scene (video), Cueball is holding up a  (a self-contained semiportable stereo system, typically with cassette tape or CD player, and complete with integrated speakers) that is playing music while Cueball declares his love for Megan. She first is startled, embarrassed, then eventually confused by the 1990 hit single  (video) by  playing on the boombox. Cueball then admits he is "not very good at this," attempting to recreate the classic romantic scene, but utterly failing to play music suitable for such an event. The film scene is also referenced and parodied in the  episode 'Raisins', when Stan unsuccessfully tries to win back his ex-girlfriend Wendy in this fashion. Stan is advised to play Peter Gabriel out of a boombox, but plays the decidedly unromantic Peter Gabriel track . It is possible that Randall was also referencing that South Park scene in this comic.

The title text mentions that Cueball is actually playing "" (video), the 1981 hit song by  and , but Megan has confused it with "Ice Ice Baby," which samples the bassline and piano motif from "Under Pressure." It also may be that Cueball is having trouble holding the boombox, hence Cueball being 'under pressure.'

These two songs are again referenced together in 1561: Water Phase Diagram, and "Ice Ice Baby" is also lampooned in 210: 90's Flowchart. It is possible that this is the same couple as mentioned in 112: Baring My Heart, as the creator of that Venn diagram reveals to their romantic partner that their feelings towards their paramour are comparable to their feelings for Vanilla Ice.
